new_exact: [¶1] Cody Atkins appeals from orders denying his application for postconviction relief
note: Orphaned line 37 in DB ('from orders denying his application') is separated from [¶1] by blank line, breaking the sentence. PDF shows these are continuous: '[¶1] Cody Atkins appeals from orders denying his application for postconviction relief'. This is a critical ingestion defect where text was misplaced during OCR/import, splitting the opening sentence of the first paragraph. The fix merges the orphaned text into the correct location in [¶1], restoring the complete sentence: 'Cody Atkins appeals from orders denying his application for postconviction relief and concluding he is a vexatious litigant.'

old_exact: Under N.D.C.C. § 28-32-28: A An administrative agency
note: Paragraph 14: Doubled article. The DB contains 'A An administrative agency' when the authoritative PDF clearly shows only 'An administrative agency' beginning the statute quote. The 'A' is a section header that belongs on its own line before [¶14], not embedded in the statute text. The simplest fix is to remove the extra 'A' article.
